raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should \"Check this\" mean primarily checking technical correctness of the research claims, checking writing clarity/polish, or checking both?","rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation plausibly requires checking whether the stated motivation, method, proof sketch, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The artifact makes concrete experimental and theoretical claims, so checking whether reported evidence supports those claims is centrally implicated.","R1.6":"A short request to check a paper passage commonly includes sentence-level clarity, grammar, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.","RX.2":"The projection should avoid inflating the requested responsibility beyond what \"Check this\" clearly supports.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within responsibility assignment and not perform the requested check itself.","RX.4":"The projection should account for possible downstream harm from approving unsupported scientific claims.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the wording of the delegation and the claims present in the artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should "Check this" mean primarily checking technical correctness of the research claims, checking writing clarity/polish, or checking both?

rationale.R1.2: The delegation plausibly requires checking whether the stated motivation, method, proof sketch, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes concrete experimental and theoretical claims, so checking whether reported evidence supports those claims is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: A short request to check a paper passage commonly includes sentence-level clarity, grammar, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The projection should avoid inflating the requested responsibility beyond what "Check this" clearly supports.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment and not perform the requested check itself.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for possible downstream harm from approving unsupported scientific claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the wording of the delegation and the claims present in the artifact.
